passthrough.due: OR-20-S: 15th day of the month following the federal due date — April 15 for calendar-year filers. OR-65: 'Calendar-year filers — returns and payments are due March 15.'
passthrough.extension: S corporation: federal Form 7004 included with the Oregon return; extended due date is the 15th day of the month following the federal extended due date (October 15 for calendar-year filers). Partnership: 'A valid federal extension is a valid extension for Oregon' — mark the Extension box; extension payment by the original due date via Revenue Online or Form OR-65-V (DOR tax calendar lists September 15, 2026 for calendar-year OR-65 extension filing).
passthrough.sourceLabel: Oregon DOR — Corporation requirements (OR-20/OR-20-S) and Partnership information (OR-65)

notes: Partnership rules at https://www.oregon.gov/dor/programs/businesses/pages/partnership.aspx; DOR Tax Calendar at https://www.oregon.gov/dor/pages/tax-calendar.aspx. Oregon's 'month after federal' pattern makes its C corp date (May 15) a month later than most states — good differentiator content.

individual.due: April 15 (DPO-05: "2025 Annual Personal Income Tax Returns - Residents, Nonresidents, Part-Year Residents" due Apr. 15, 2026)
individual.extension: Up to six months — extended return due October 15 (DPO-05: "2025 Annual Personal Income Tax Return with six-month extension" on Oct. 15). PA accepts a federal extension if no PA tax is owed with the return; otherwise file Form REV-276 (per PA DOR "Extension of Time to File" page, https://www.pa.gov/agencies/revenue/resources/tax-types-and-information/personal-income-tax/extension-of-time-to-file). Extension does not extend time to pay.
individual.sourceLabel: PA DOR — 2026 State Tax Due Date Reference Guide (DPO-05)

corporate.form: RCT-101 (PA Corporate Net Income Tax Report)
corporate.due: May 15 for calendar-year filers (DPO-05: "Annual CNI Corporation Tax Report for previous tax year ending Dec. 31" listed on May 15, 2026)
corporate.extension: Per Form REV-853: "Taxpayers granted an extension to file the federal return will automatically be granted an extension to file RCT-101" (indicate on RCT-101 and include a copy of the federal extension); without a federal extension, REV-853 requests "a Pennsylvania extension of up to 60 days." Payment still due by the original due date. (Source: REV-853, https://www.pa.gov/content/dam/copapwp-pagov/en/revenue/documents/formsandpublications/formsforbusinesses/corporationtax/documents/rev-853.pdf)
corporate.sourceLabel: PA DOR — 2026 State Tax Due Date Reference Guide (DPO-05)

passthrough.form: PA-20S/PA-65 (PA S Corporation/Partnership Information Return)
passthrough.due: April 15 for calendar-year filers; when the year ends in any other month, "the PA-20S/PA-65 Information Return is due on the 15th day of the fourth month following the close of the fiscal year" (DPO-05)
passthrough.extension: Five-month extension — extended return due September 15 (DPO-05: "Annual 2025 PA S Corporation/Partnership Information Return (PA-20S/PA-65) with five-month extension" on Sept. 15, 2026)
passthrough.sourceLabel: PA DOR — 2026 State Tax Due Date Reference Guide (DPO-05)

notes: PA CNIT quarterly estimates and S-election (REV-976) dates also listed in DPO-05. Nonresident withholding installments for PA S corps/partnerships due with PA-20S/PA-65.

individual.due: April 15 ("A personal income tax return must be computed and filed on or before the 15th day of the fourth month following the close of a taxable year"; Form RI-4868 confirms filing/payment on or before April 15, 2026 for tax year 2025)
individual.extension: "Use Form RI-4868 to obtain an automatic 6 month extension of time to file" (to October 15 for calendar-year filers); file and pay by April 15, 2026; "the Rhode Island extension form need not be filed if you are not required to make payment with Rhode Island extension form" (federal extension honored in that case); no extension of time to pay (2025 Form RI-4868, https://tax.ri.gov/sites/g/files/xkgbur541/files/2026-01/2025%20RI-4868_w.pdf)
individual.sourceLabel: RI Division of Taxation — Personal Income Tax

corporate.due: April 15 for calendar-year filers ("For calendar year and non-June 30 fiscal year end filers, Form RI-1120C is due on or before the fifteenth day of the fourth month following the close of the taxable year")
corporate.extension: Form RI-7004, "Automatic Extension Request for RI-1120C, RI-1120S and RI-1065 Filers": "automatic six (6) month extension for filers of Form RI-1120C (except for filers with a June 30 fiscal year end)"; seven months for June 30 year-ends; full estimated tax payment must accompany the request (https://tax.ri.gov/sites/g/files/xkgbur541/files/2022-01/ri-7004_1d_w.pdf)
corporate.sourceLabel: RI Division of Taxation — Corporate Tax Filing Requirements

passthrough.form: RI-1120S (S corporation) / RI-1065 (partnership)
passthrough.due: March 15 for calendar-year filers ("Forms RI-1065 and RI-1120S are due on or before the fifteenth day of the third month following the close of the taxable year"); exception: single-member LLC RI-1065 filers use the fifteenth day of the fourth month
passthrough.extension: Form RI-7004 — "automatic six (6) month extension" for RI-1120S and RI-1065 filers; payment of the full amount of tax reasonably estimated to be due must be submitted with the request (https://tax.ri.gov/sites/g/files/xkgbur541/files/2022-01/ri-7004_1d_w.pdf)
passthrough.sourceLabel: RI Division of Taxation — Corporate Tax Filing Requirements

notes: RI-4868 warns it does NOT extend RI-1040H (property tax relief) or RI-6238 — those must be filed by April 15, 2026 even with an extension.

individual.due: April 15 (April 15, 2026 for TY2025). SPECIAL FOR TY2025: on March 26 the SCDOR extended the filing due date for all 2025 SC Individual Income Tax returns to October 15, 2026 (IRC-conformity legislation, H.B. 3368); payment was NOT extended — at least 90% of 2025 liability was due April 15, 2026
individual.extension: Form SC4868 (or pay balance on MyDORWAY) for extension to file; for TY2025 the October 15, 2026 filing extension is automatic per the SCDOR statement; "This extension applies only to the deadline to file your return, not to pay what you owe"
individual.sourceLabel: SCDOR statement on Income Tax conformity; April 15 filing deadline extended for SC returns

corporate.due: "All corporate taxable entities (other than S Corporations and Tax Exempt Organizations) file annually on or before the 15th day of the fourth month after the close of the taxable year" (April 15 for calendar-year filers)
corporate.extension: File the Tentative Corporate Tax Return (SC1120-T) with payment by the original due date (paying on MyDORWAY submits the extension request automatically); there is no extension of time to pay; extended date not stated on the page
corporate.sourceLabel: SCDOR — Corporate tax page

passthrough.form: Form SC1120S (S corporation) / Form SC1065 (partnership)
passthrough.due: Both due the "15th day of the third month after the end of your business year" (March 15 for calendar-year filers) — stated on the SCDOR S Corporation page (SC1120S) and Partnership page (SC1065)
passthrough.extension: S corp: request extension via SC1120-T / MyDORWAY payment ("Your payment on MyDORWAY automatically submits your filing extension request"); no extension of time to pay. Partnership: Form SC8736 — "The request for extension extends the filing due date for up to six months" (to September 15); no extension of time to pay
passthrough.sourceLabel: SCDOR — Partnership page (SC1065); S Corporation page at dor.sc.gov/business-income-taxes/corporate/s-corporation

notes: Normal-year quirk: SC1040 filers who both file AND pay electronically have until May 1 without penalty or interest (per SCDOR 'Tax Season opens January 26, 2026', dor.sc.gov/news/tax-season-opens-january-26-2026). TY2025 individual returns auto-extended to October 15, 2026 (filing only). SC1120S includes the annual report (Schedule D) and license fee.

individual.sourceLabel: South Dakota DOR — Individuals: "South Dakota is one of seven states that does not impose a state income tax"

corporate.sourceLabel: South Dakota DOR — Business Tax: "South Dakota does not impose a corporate income tax"

passthrough.sourceLabel: South Dakota DOR — Business Tax (no corporate or personal income tax; pass-throughs file no state income return)

notes: South Dakota has NO individual or corporate income tax — the absence is the fact. Main business tax obligations: state sales & use tax and contractor's excise tax (license-based, filed via the DOR portal). Financial institutions only: bank franchise tax return, due within 15 days after the federal income tax return is due; extension requires a cover letter, a copy of the federal extension request, and a minimum $200 estimated payment by the original due date (source: https://dor.sd.gov/businesses/taxes/bank-franchise-tax/).

individual.due: Not applicable — Tennessee has no individual income tax on wages. The Hall income tax (on interest and dividends only; it never applied to salaries, wages, and commissions) was repealed for tax periods beginning January 1, 2021 or later
individual.sourceLabel: Tennessee DOR — Hall Income Tax (repealed)

corporate.form: Form FAE170 (Franchise and Excise Tax Return)
corporate.due: "15th day of the fourth month following the close of your books and records. For businesses with a January 1 - December 31 calendar year, this tax is due on April 15 of the following year"
corporate.extension: Seven-month extension (as stated on the TN DOR Franchise & Excise due dates page); extension payments can be made through TNTAP
corporate.sourceLabel: Tennessee DOR — Franchise & Excise Tax: Due Dates and Tax Rates

passthrough.form: Form FAE170 (Franchise and Excise Tax Return) — same return as corporations
passthrough.due: Same rule: 15th day of the fourth month following the close of books and records (April 15 for calendar-year entities). Tennessee has no pass-through income tax return for owners; "If you are a corporation, limited partnership, limited liability company, or business trust chartered, qualified, or registered in Tennessee or doing business in this state, then you must register for and pay franchise and excise taxes"
passthrough.extension: Seven-month extension, same as corporate (Franchise & Excise due dates page)
passthrough.sourceLabel: Tennessee DOR — Franchise & Excise Tax overview

notes: No-income-tax state for individuals (wage income): main business tax obligation is the Franchise & Excise Tax — franchise tax on net worth ($100 minimum), excise tax on net earnings. General partnerships and sole proprietorships are not listed among the entities required to register on the overview page; must-file filing is online via TNTAP

individual.due: No state personal income tax — Comptroller: "Texas does not have a personal income tax, and sole proprietorships, the state's most common form of new business, are completely exempt from the franchise tax, its main business tax."
individual.sourceLabel: Texas Comptroller — Fiscal Notes: Starting a New Business

corporate.due: "The annual franchise tax report is due May 15. If May 15 falls on a weekend or holiday, the due date will be the next business day." Applies to "each taxable entity formed or organized in Texas or doing business in Texas."
corporate.extension: Form 05-164 Texas Franchise Tax Extension Request (or Webfile/EFT) filed with payment by the original due date — "Aug. 15 is the extended due date" (first extension); "Nov. 15 is the extended due date" (second extension, mandatory-EFT payers). To be valid, "100 percent of the tax paid in the prior year, or 90 percent of the tax that will be due with the current year's report, must be paid on or before the original due date."
corporate.sourceLabel: Texas Comptroller — Franchise Tax / Filing Extensions

passthrough.form: Texas Franchise Tax Report (same report for all taxable entities, including S corporations, partnerships, and LLCs)
passthrough.due: "The annual franchise tax report is due May 15. If May 15 falls on a weekend or holiday, the due date will be the next business day." The franchise tax applies to "each taxable entity formed or organized in Texas or doing business in Texas" — Texas does not recognize federal S-corp/partnership pass-through treatment for franchise-tax purposes.
passthrough.extension: Same as corporate: extension request with payment by May 15; "Aug. 15 is the extended due date"; second extension to "Nov. 15" (extension detail source: https://comptroller.texas.gov/taxes/franchise/filing-extensions.php)
passthrough.sourceLabel: Texas Comptroller — Franchise Tax

notes: Texas has no personal income tax; the franchise tax is the main state business tax and one May 15 report covers C corps, S corps, partnerships, and LLCs alike. Sole proprietorships (and certain general partnerships of natural persons) are exempt. Extension rules quoted from the Comptroller's filing-extensions page (https://comptroller.texas.gov/taxes/franchise/filing-extensions.php).

individual.due: April 15 ("by April 15, 2026, if you file on a calendar year basis (tax year ends Dec. 31, 2025)"; fiscal-year filers: 15th day of the fourth month after the fiscal year ends)
individual.extension: "You get an automatic extension of up to six months to file your return. You do not need to file an extension form" — all extension returns must be filed by Oct. 15, 2026. Not an extension to pay: prepay 90% of the current-year tax or 100% of the previous year's liability by the original due date
individual.sourceLabel: Utah State Tax Commission — Income Tax: Extensions and Prepayments (incometax.utah.gov)

corporate.due: "A return must be filed on or before the 15th day of the fourth month following the close of the tax year or the due date of the federal return, whichever is later" (April 15 for calendar-year filers)
corporate.extension: "Corporations are automatically allowed an extension of up to six months to file a return without filing an extension form" — not an extension to pay; extension prepayments must equal 90% of current-year tax (or the $100 minimum tax) or 100% of the previous year's liability
corporate.sourceLabel: Utah State Tax Commission — 2025 TC-20 Utah Corporation Franchise and Income Tax Return instructions

passthrough.form: Form TC-20S (S corporation) / Form TC-65 (partnership)
passthrough.due: Both: "on or before the 15th day of the fourth month following the close of the tax year or the due date of the federal return, whichever is later" (April 15 for calendar-year filers — note Utah pass-through returns are due in the fourth month, not the federal third month)
passthrough.extension: TC-20S: automatic extension of up to six months without filing a form; TC-65: "Partnerships are automatically allowed an extension of up to five months to file a return without filing an extension form." Not an extension to pay — prepayment requirements apply, and TC-65 pass-through withholding must be paid by the original due date
passthrough.sourceLabel: Utah State Tax Commission — 2025 TC-65 Utah Partnership/LLC/LLP instructions (S-corp: 2025 TC-20S instructions)

notes: All Utah income-tax due dates (TC-40, TC-20, TC-20S, TC-65) fall on April 15 for calendar-year filers, confirmed on the Tax Commission due-date calendar (https://tax.utah.gov/event/individual-corporate-partnership-income-tax-due-date-jan-dec-2025/). All extensions are automatic (no form) but conditioned on prepayment; the partnership extension is five months (aligning with the federal Sept. 15 extended date) versus six months for corporations. TC-20S instructions: https://tax.utah.gov/forms/current/tc-20sinst.pdf

individual.due: April 15 ("The filing deadline is April 15, 2026" for tax year 2025; Vermont's personal income tax filing season follows the federal calendar)
individual.extension: Six-month extension (to October 15), but taxpayers must submit an application first: file online through myVTax or submit Form IN-151 by the April due date. Vermont also allows an automatic extension upon extension at the federal level. Extension of time to file only — tax due must be paid by the April deadline.
individual.sourceLabel: Vermont Department of Taxes — Filing Season FAQs (Tax Year 2025)

corporate.due: "Due on the date prescribed for filing under the Internal Revenue Code" — April 15, 2026 for corporations using the calendar year (per the department's due-date table)
corporate.extension: Form BA-403, Application for Extension of Time to File Vermont Corporate/Business Income Tax Return (due with the April 15 deadline). An extension of time to file a federal return automatically extends the time to file with Vermont until 30 days beyond the federal extension date; tax is due on the original due date. Extended due date for calendar-year CO-411: October 15, 2026.
corporate.sourceLabel: Vermont Department of Taxes — Corporate Income Tax

passthrough.form: Form BI-471 (Business Income Tax Return) for S corporations, partnerships, and LLCs taxed as pass-throughs; Form BI-476 (Resident Only) for entities owned exclusively by Vermont residents
passthrough.due: "Due on the date established for filing under the Internal Revenue Code" — March 16, 2026 for calendar-year S corporations, partnerships, and LLCs (March 15 falls on a Sunday)
passthrough.extension: Form BA-403 by the original due date. A federal extension automatically extends the Vermont filing deadline until 30 days beyond the federal extension date; tax is due on the original due date. Extended due date for calendar-year BI-471/BI-476: October 15, 2026.
passthrough.sourceLabel: Vermont Department of Taxes — Business Entity Income Tax

notes: Pass-through entities generally owe a $250 minimum entity tax with BI-471/BI-476. Entities must make quarterly nonresident-withholding estimated payments (Form WH-435); composite filing is mandatory for pass-throughs with more than 50 nonresident owners. The department's extension page notes Vermont gives corporations one additional month to file upon a federal extension.

individual.due: May 1 ("Typically, most people must file their tax return by May 1"); fiscal-year filers: 15th day of the 4th month after year-end
individual.extension: "Virginia allows an automatic 6-month extension to file your return (Nov. 1 for most filers)" — no application required; tax owed must still be paid by the original May 1 due date
individual.sourceLabel: Virginia Tax — When to File

corporate.due: April 15 for calendar-year filers; "15th day of the 4th month following the close of your taxable year" for fiscal-year filers
corporate.extension: "An automatic 7-month filing extension. Calendar year filers have an extended due date of Nov. 15" (nonprofits: automatic 6-month); "the extension provisions do not apply to payment of any tax due" — pay at least 90% by the original due date
corporate.sourceLabel: Virginia Tax — Corporation Income Tax

passthrough.form: Form 502 (S corporations and partnerships); Form 502PTET for electing PTEs
passthrough.due: "PTE income tax returns are due by the 15th day of the 4th month following the close of the business's taxable year" (April 15 for calendar-year entities)
passthrough.extension: Automatic 6-month extension, no application required — "you must file Form 502 no later than 6 months from the original due date" (October 15 for calendar-year filers); withholding tax payment is not extended
passthrough.sourceLabel: Virginia Tax — Pass-Through Entities

notes: Virginia's individual due date (May 1) and extended date (Nov 1) are later than the federal dates. Elective PTET filed on Form 502PTET.

individual.due: No state income tax — Washington DOR: "Washington state does not have an individual or corporate income tax."
individual.sourceLabel: Washington DOR — Income tax

corporate.form: Excise Tax Return (business & occupation tax, filed via MyDOR)
corporate.due: By assigned filing frequency — "Monthly returns are due the 25th of every month"; "Quarterly returns are due the end of the month following the tax quarter"; "Annual returns are due April 15." If a due date falls on a weekend or legal holiday, it extends to the next business day.
corporate.sourceLabel: Washington DOR — Filing frequencies & due dates

passthrough.form: Excise Tax Return (business & occupation tax) — same return regardless of entity type
passthrough.due: Same as corporate: monthly returns due the 25th of the following month; quarterly returns due the end of the month following the tax quarter; annual returns due April 15
passthrough.sourceLabel: Washington DOR — Filing frequencies & due dates

notes: No individual or corporate income tax; businesses instead owe B&O gross-receipts tax ("calculated on the total value of products sold or the total income the business earns" with no deduction for expenses; source: https://dor.wa.gov/taxes-rates/business-occupation-tax). Capital gains tax: "a 7% tax on the sale or exchange of long-term capital assets" owed by individuals only; "Individuals must file their capital gains tax return by the same day as the individual's federal income tax return is due" (typically April 15); filing extension available via My DOR if a federal extension was received, but "A filing extension does not extend the due date for submitting a payment" (source: https://dor.wa.gov/taxes-rates/other-taxes/capital-gains-tax).

individual.due: April 15 ("April 15 is the final day to file a 2025 tax return, request an extension, and pay tax owed")
individual.extension: Six-month extension to October 15 ("October 15 is the final day to file 2025 tax returns with approved extension"); the extension request (WV4868, part of the IT-140 packet) must be filed before the original due date; an extension to file is not an extension to pay — payment due April 15
individual.sourceLabel: WV Tax Division — Income Tax Filing Season

corporate.due: "Annual returns will have a due date of April 15 and an extended due date of October 15. Fiscal and 52/53-week returns will be due on the 15th day of the fourth month following the end of the period"
corporate.extension: Six-month extension period. "An extension of time to file a federal return is automatically accepted by West Virginia as an extension of time to file the West Virginia return" (attach federal Form 7004); a state extension may be obtained via Form CIT-120EXT even without a federal one; does not extend time to pay
corporate.sourceLabel: WV Tax Division — 2025 CIT-120 Instructions

passthrough.form: Form PTE-100 (S Corporation & Partnership Income Tax Return)
passthrough.due: "Annual returns will have a due date of March 15... and an extended due date of September 15. Fiscal and 52/53-week returns will be due on the 15th day of the third month following the close of the taxable year with an extension period of six months"
passthrough.extension: Six-month extension (to September 15 for calendar-year filers); an extension of time for filing does not extend the time for payment
passthrough.sourceLabel: WV Tax Division — PTE-100 Income Tax for Pass-Through Entities Instructions

notes: PTE-100 due-date wording is from the PTE-100 instructions ('for periods beginning on or after January 1, 2022'); one form covers both S corporations and partnerships.

individual.due: "The due date for filing the return is the date required to file the corresponding federal income tax return or April 15."
individual.extension: Any federal extension automatically extends the Wisconsin due date to the federal extended due date (October 15 with federal Form 4868). A Wisconsin-only extension is available even without filing with the IRS — attach a copy of the federal extension application or a statement to the Wisconsin return. Not an extension to pay: unpaid tax accrues 1% interest per month during the extension.
individual.sourceLabel: Wisconsin DOR Publication 401 — Extensions of Time to File (01/26)

corporate.form: Form 4 / Form 6 (Corporation franchise or income tax return)
corporate.due: 15th day of the 4th month after end of taxable year (fiscal year ending June 30: 15th day of the 3rd month)
corporate.extension: A federal extension extends the Wisconsin due date to 30 days after the federal extended due date; alternatively, "Wisconsin law provides an automatic extension of seven months or until the original due date of the corporation's corresponding federal return, whichever is later." Payment still due by the original date (interest applies).
corporate.sourceLabel: Wisconsin DOR Publication 401 — Extensions of Time to File (01/26)

passthrough.form: Form 5S (tax-option (S) corporation franchise or income tax return) / Form 3 (partnership return)
passthrough.due: Form 5S: 15th day of the 3rd month after end of taxable year. Form 3: 15th day of the 3rd month after the end of the taxable year.
passthrough.extension: Form 5S follows the corporation rules (federal extension + 30 days, or automatic Wisconsin 7-month extension). Form 3: a federal extension automatically extends the Wisconsin return to the federal extended due date; Wisconsin-only extension available by enclosing a federal extension copy or statement. Tax not paid by the original due date accrues 1% interest per month.
passthrough.sourceLabel: Wisconsin DOR Publication 401 — Extensions of Time to File (01/26)

notes: Nonresident composite returns: Form 1CNS (S corp shareholders) and Form 1CNP (partners) are due the 15th day of the 4th month after the end of the calendar taxable year. Publication 401 (01/26) states it is for preparing 2025 returns.

agency: Wyoming Secretary of State (annual report license tax); Wyoming Department of Revenue (excise, mineral, and property taxes)

individual.sourceLabel: State of Wyoming — About Wyoming
individual.note: Wyoming has NO state individual income tax — 'Unlike other states, Wyoming does not possess an individual or corporate income tax.' No individual return is filed.

corporate.form: Annual Report with annual report license tax — filed with the Wyoming Secretary of State (Wyoming has no corporate income tax)
corporate.due: 'The annual report due date for all registered business entities (with the exception of Statutory Trusts) is based on the anniversary month of formation' — due on the first day of the anniversary month (e.g., initial filing May 15 → report due May 1 each year); Statutory Trust annual reports are due January 1
corporate.sourceLabel: Wyoming Secretary of State — Business Division FAQs (due date and license tax) / wyobiz.wyo.gov Annual Report page
corporate.note: License tax: 'The tax is $60 or two-tenths of one mill on the dollar ($.0002), whichever is greater,' based on assets located and employed in Wyoming; entities with $300,000 or less in Wyoming assets pay the $60 minimum. No extension provision is stated on the official pages.

passthrough.sourceLabel: State of Wyoming — About Wyoming (no income tax); Wyoming SOS Annual Report page (annual report applies to LLCs/LPs/RLLPs)
passthrough.note: Wyoming has no state income tax, so S corporations and partnerships file no state income tax return. LLCs, LPs, RLLPs (and corporations) all file the same Secretary of State annual report due the first day of their anniversary month of formation.

notes: The absence of individual and corporate income tax is the fact (source: https://www.wyo.gov/about-wyoming). Main recurring business obligation is the SOS annual report license tax. Wyoming DOR (https://revenue.wyo.gov/) administers excise/sales & use, mineral, and property taxes only.
